title: MandoMedic & the Responder Toolkit App
description:
﻿﻿﻿On this second episode with MandoMedic, we discuss the new app he built: Responder Toolkit. It's a mental health recourse designed for the needs of the first responder and veteran communities.
Check it out at respondertoolkit.com
And follow Mando on Instagram: @TheMandoMedic

episode webpage: archive.org/details/mando-medic-the-responder-toolkit-app

www.rykerrmedical.com to see what else is going on with the project

title: The Combat Midwife Talks OB/GYN for EMS
description:
﻿﻿﻿Jessica Arno is a midwife, paramedic, and battlefield-born educator who's trained over 100,000 combat medics, Special Warfare medics, and EMS professionals in OB/GYN and pediatric emergencies. From Afghan refugee camps to rural fire stations, she equips medics and civilians with the skills to handle birth, bleeding, and babies - when help isn't coming. Her fast-paced, no-fluff training is changing the face of prehospital care - one confident responder at a time.
Connect with the The Combat Midwife:
Website: combatmidwife.com
Discount Code: RYKERR
show notes for the episode with links to everything we discuss: rykerrmedical.com/not-linked/show-notes-combat-midwife-podcast
episode webpage: archive.org/details/the-combat-midwife

title: Kevin Hazzard & His Books!
description:
Kevin Hazzard is a former paramedic, soon-to-be nurse, and author of both 1000 Naked Strangers and American Sirens. His newest book, No One's Coming, is out soon, so we brought him on to discuss that and to catch up on things in general.
As a sidenote: there is a YouTube version of this show that includes a bit more informal chitchat between Kevin and Ryan, feel free to check that out if you feel inclined.
Kevin's website is www.kevinhazzard.com; you can also follow him on instagram, @goes_by_hazzard
episode webpage: archive.org/details/kevin-hazard-his-books

www.rykerrmedical.com to see what else is going on with the project

title: Dan Taylor Chats Non-Traditional Paramedic Work
description:
Dan Taylor is a remote and critical care paramedic who has spent most of his career providing medical care and education in remote and austere settings around the world. Specifically, he has extensive experience in hazardous and conflict environments. On this episode, Dan and Ryan discuss the intricacies of working in this space and how one might go about getting a foot in the door if interested.
Dan's website is frozenmedical.com and he has all sorts of resources on that page. You can also follow him on instagram, @frozenmedical
Notes on everything we discuss in the episode: rykerrmedical.com/not-linked/show-notes-dan-taylor-podcast
episode webpage: archive.org/details/dan-taylor-chats-non-traditional-paramedic-work
rykerrmedical.com to see what else is going on with the project

title: Drew and Ryan Discuss EMS Education at SFCC and in NM
description:
Drew is the Director at the EMS Institute at Santa Fe Community College. He and Ryan discuss, among many other things, a critical care transport course they have recently rolled out, how EMSI and SFCC approach EMS education given unique challenges in New Mexico, the state of EMS training in New Mexico as a whole, and incorporating ultrasound training into initial paramedic training programs.
Learn more by following the EMSI on IG at @sfcc_ems_institute, by checking out their webpage at sfcc.edu/programs/paramedicine or by contacting Drew directly: drew.congdon@sfcc.edu

episode webpage: archive.org/details/drew-and-ryan-discuss-ems-education-at-sfcc-and-in-nm
rykerrmedical.com to see what else is going on with the project

title: A Conversation with Flight Nurse Morgan Holmes
description:
Morgan is an ICU nurse turned flight nurse out in NM. Ryan asks him some questions about how it's been getting into flight and what he's up to with a new project built to help others navigate the transition. Morgan then asks Ryan about the work he's been up to outside of the CCT world.
Morgan can be found on IG at @criticalcarecommunity
His project is at: skool.com/flight-nurse-mentoring-6195/about
episode webpage: archive.org/details/a-conversation-with-flight-nurse-morgan-holmes

title: Ukraine Chat with Sarah and Jason
description:
Sarah is a brand-new EMT currently working in Ukraine with an NGO called CADUS. Jason is a nurse/ medic who worked with her over there earlier this year through GRM, another NGO. This is a conversation the three of us had to both catch up on things and share the story with you.

title: Sleep Discussion with MandoMedic
description:
In this episode we discuss strategies to improve and optimize sleep while working 24 and 48 hour shifts as we often do in EMS. Our guest is MandoMedic; he hails from the European Medics community and you can find him either on their Discord channel or on Instagram @TheMandoMedic.
episode webpage: archive.org/details/sleep-discussion-with-mando-medic_202502

title: Patient Assessment in EMS and CCT
description:
This episode is just one voice chatting about how we do patient assessment in EMS, how that differs from the way we do it in nursing, and then how we can come up with an ideal blend to put to work in the critical care transport setting.
episode webpage: archive.org/details/patient-assessment-in-ems-and-cct

title: EMS in Haiti with Claudel and Armadeus
description:
The primary story here is getting Claudel to medic school in order to become Haiti's first paramedic. He needs your support, so check it out and see what can be done. We also mention some other cool stuff along the way. www.empacthaiti.org. And as always, let us know what you think!
episode webpage: archive.org/details/ems-in-haiti-with-claudel-and-armadeus_202502

title: Scripts Discussion with Richard
description:
In this episode we discuss the idea of creating scripts for learning protocols and refining procedures with Richard Templeman, a flight paramedic in Nevada. We talk about the development of the idea, outline how we've used it personally, and provide examples of how you can do the same in your own clinical practice. Let us know what you think and reach out with any questions.
episode webpage: archive.org/details/scripts-discussion-with-richard_202502
